tasks.title: Redact webhook URL logging to avoid leaking secrets
evidence: crates/ralph/src/webhook.rs:15-18 defines invariant 'Secrets are never logged or exposed in error messages'
crates/ralph/src/webhook.rs:157-162 logs the full webhook URL on success (log::debug! with url)
crates/ralph/src/webhook.rs:186-190 logs error details from send_request, which can include URL context
plan: Introduce a URL redaction helper (e.g., keep scheme/host, drop userinfo/query/fragment) for webhook logs
Replace webhook success/failure log statements to use redacted URL or omit URL entirely
Add unit tests for the redaction helper to cover URLs with embedded tokens/query params
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph webhook` and confirming logs avoid raw URLs
notes: Principles: I) Security baseline, G) Least astonishment

tasks.title: Handle missing session file in timeout recovery without panic
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:149-163 calls session::load_session(...).unwrap() in Timeout branch
plan: Update timeout recovery to handle Ok(None) from load_session (return clean error or treat as NoSession)
Add a regression test that simulates a missing session file after Timeout validation (ensure no panic)
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph run` and confirming timeout path handles missing session safely
notes: Principles: E) Make bugs hard to write, F) Fail fast appropriately

tasks.title: Make queue JSON repair bracket counting string-aware
evidence: crates/ralph/src/queue.rs:203-215 counts brackets/braces with raw string matches before appending closers
Bracket counting ignores string literals, so embedded '[' or '{' in task fields can trigger incorrect repairs
plan: Replace raw bracket/brace counting with a string-aware scanner (reuse repair_unescaped_newlines state machine or a JSON parser pass)
Add tests where queue JSON contains bracket/brace characters inside string fields and ensure no extra closers are appended
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph queue::repair` and ensuring repaired output remains valid
notes: Principles: E) Make bugs hard to write, F) Fail fast appropriately

tasks.title: Split oversized TUI app module into cohesive submodules
evidence: wc -l crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs -> 2972 lines (exceeds 1000 LOC ceiling)
crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs:1-21 documents state + rendering + input + session setup responsibilities in one module
plan: Identify cohesive slices (state model, event loop, rendering, persistence) within the TUI app module
Extract each slice into dedicated `crates/ralph/src/tui/app_*` or `crates/ralph/src/tui/app/` submodules and wire through `tui/mod.rs`
Move or add unit tests for extracted helpers to keep behavior coverage intact
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph tui` and confirming no TUI behavior regressions
notes: Principles: D) Localize change, A) KISS, E) Make bugs hard to write

tasks.title: Break up run command test mega-module into focused test files
evidence: wc -l crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs -> 2299 lines (exceeds 1000 LOC ceiling)
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs:1-12 shows this single file houses all run orchestration unit tests
plan: Group run-command tests by concern (agent overrides, iteration settings, completion signals, logging) and map target modules
Split `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs` into multiple submodules under `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests/` and update `mod` wiring
Ensure each new test module keeps or improves coverage for its concern area
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph run` and confirming all run-related tests pass
notes: Principles: D) Localize change, A) KISS

tasks.title: Decompose config contracts into smaller submodules
evidence: wc -l crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs -> 1486 lines (exceeds 1000 LOC ceiling)
crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs:1-13 shows this file defines all config contracts + defaults in one module
plan: Identify config domains (queue, agent, parallel, tui, notifications, etc.) that can be isolated
Split `config.rs` into submodules under `crates/ralph/src/contracts/config/` and re-export a stable public surface
Add or relocate unit tests to the new submodules where behavior is defined
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph config` and `make generate` to confirm schema output remains aligned
notes: Principles: D) Localize change, C) DRY (knowledge)

tasks.title: Refactor parallel run supervisor into smaller modules
evidence: wc -l crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs -> 1411 lines (exceeds 1000 LOC ceiling)
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:1-16 lists worker selection, PR creation, and cleanup responsibilities in one file
plan: Carve out submodules for worker lifecycle, merge handling, and cleanup/state tracking
Move related structs/functions into `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/` submodules and keep the public entrypoint stable
Add or move tests to cover extracted helpers as needed
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph parallel` (or `cargo test -p ralph run` if no dedicated tests)
notes: Principles: D) Localize change, A) KISS

tasks.title: Reduce run orchestrator size below 1000 LOC and refresh stale size note
evidence: wc -l crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs -> 1315 lines (exceeds 1000 LOC ceiling)
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:18-24 claims size justification at 1002 LOC, now stale
plan: Identify self-contained orchestration chunks (pre-run CI gate, queue locking, supervision) suitable for extraction
Extract those chunks into dedicated submodules and update the `commands/run/mod.rs` interface
Update the size justification comment to reflect the new module size and scope
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph run` and confirming run workflow coverage still passes

Ensure tests covering model/runner parsing remain colocated with the extracted helpers
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph agent` (or `cargo test -p ralph run` if tests are shared)

tasks.title: Refactor watch command into watcher/detector/notification modules
evidence: wc -l crates/ralph/src/commands/watch.rs -> 1082 lines (exceeds 1000 LOC ceiling)
crates/ralph/src/commands/watch.rs:1-21 shows watcher setup, debounce, detection, notification, and task creation in one file
plan: Identify logical areas (watcher setup, debounce bookkeeping, comment detection, notifications) and define submodule boundaries
Extract helpers into `crates/ralph/src/commands/watch/` submodules and keep the CLI entrypoint stable
Ensure detection logic and debounce behavior remain covered by unit tests
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph watch` and confirming watch tests pass
notes: Principles: D) Localize change, A) KISS

tasks.title: Use real RFC3339 parsing for execution history weighting
evidence: crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs:229-260 uses 30-day months and 365-day years to compute seconds
This approximation skews age weighting for ETA calculations, especially across month boundaries
plan: Replace parse_timestamp_to_secs with timeutil::parse_rfc3339 and OffsetDateTime::unix_timestamp()
Add tests covering month boundary and leap-day timestamps to validate correct epoch conversion
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph execution_history` and confirming weighting math stays stable
notes: Principles: A) KISS, E) Make bugs hard to write

tasks.title: Fix streak date arithmetic to handle real calendar boundaries
evidence: crates/ralph/src/productivity.rs:294-323 uses manual date math and defaults year/month/day on parse failure
Manual month-length handling ignores leap years and can miscompute yesterday for 1st-of-month
plan: Replace get_previous_date logic with time crate date parsing and Duration::days(1) subtraction
Handle invalid date inputs explicitly (return error or keep original date) rather than defaulting to 2026-01-01
Add tests for month boundaries and leap year transitions
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph productivity`
notes: Principles: E) Make bugs hard to write, G) Least astonishment

tasks.title: Fix velocity date offsets to use real calendar math
evidence: crates/ralph/src/productivity.rs:392-418 converts dates using 30-day months and 365-day years
Offsets can yield invalid dates and wrong day windows for velocity metrics
plan: Replace get_date_offset with time crate date arithmetic (OffsetDateTime/Date + Duration::days)
Add tests for offsets spanning month/year boundaries
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph productivity`
notes: Principles: E) Make bugs hard to write, G) Least astonishment

tasks.title: Surface git LFS health check errors instead of silently passing
evidence: crates/ralph/src/git/lfs.rs:540-542 documents Err on validation failures
crates/ralph/src/git/lfs.rs:565-572 drops errors via .ok() and .unwrap_or_default()
plan: Propagate list_lfs_files/validate_lfs_pointers errors (or capture them in LfsHealthReport)
Update tests to cover failure paths and ensure errors are surfaced
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph lfs`
notes: Principles: F) Fail fast appropriately, H) Operational hygiene

tasks.title: Remove fixed RFC3339 fallback timestamp
evidence: crates/ralph/src/timeutil.rs:67-68 returns FALLBACK_RFC3339 on formatting errors
crates/ralph/src/constants.rs:188-189 sets FALLBACK_RFC3339 to a fixed 2026-01-18 timestamp
plan: Replace fixed fallback with a dynamic safe fallback (or return Result and handle errors at call sites)
Update callers that rely on now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback to handle failures explicitly
Add tests to cover the fallback/error path
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph timeutil`
notes: Principles: F) Fail fast appropriately, G) Least astonishment

tasks.title: Stop writing empty timestamps when now_utc_rfc3339 fails
evidence: crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs:120-122 uses now_utc_rfc3339().unwrap_or_default()
crates/ralph/src/productivity.rs:334-337 and 366-367 use now_utc_rfc3339().unwrap_or_default()
plan: Replace unwrap_or_default with fallible handling (propagate error) or use a validated fallback
Add tests to confirm timestamp fields are never empty on success
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph execution_history productivity`
notes: Principles: E) Make bugs hard to write, F) Fail fast appropriately

tasks.title: Centralize max_dependency_depth default handling
evidence: rg --line-number "max_dependency_depth.unwrap_or(10)" crates/ralph/src shows many call sites
crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs:1138 sets default max_dependency_depth: Some(10)
plan: Add a helper (e.g., Resolved::max_dependency_depth()) or pre-resolve defaults once
Replace per-call unwrap_or(10) with the centralized helper
Update tests if needed to reflect new helper
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph queue`
notes: Principles: C) DRY, D) Localize change

tasks.title: Auto-respond to 401/429 errors with 'Proceed as you were' for transient provider issues
evidence: User request: When supervisor encounters 401 error with 'invalid_authentication_error' type and message 'The API Key appears to be invalid or may have expired', it should auto-respond 'Proceed as you were'
User request: Also handle 429 rate_limit_reached_error with message 'We're receiving too many requests at the moment. Please wait a moment and try again.'
Both errors are transient (provider overwhelmed), not actual invalid/expired key or true rate limits
Similar functionality exists for CI gate failure automatic response
Should apply to all phases (1, 2, 3) where these errors may occur
plan: Locate existing CI gate auto-response logic in supervisor/runner code
Identify where 401 invalid_authentication_error and 429 rate_limit_reached_error are currently detected and prompt user
Implement automatic 'Proceed as you were' response when either error pattern is detected
Ensure auto-response works across all phases (1, 2, 3)
Add tests for both 401 and 429 auto-response behaviors (success and failure modes)
Update documentation if user-facing behavior changes
Verify by running `make ci`

tasks.title: Fix phase_overrides missing from config schema default value
evidence: User request: 'Validate how schemas/config.schema.json is generated. The phase_overrides section is not being shown in the schema'
Verified: phase_overrides IS present in AgentConfig definition properties
Verified: phase_overrides IS MISSING from agent.default in schema (where default values are shown)
Root cause: phase_overrides uses #[serde(skip_serializing_if = 'Option::is_none')] which skips it during default serialization for schema
plan: Investigate how schema default values are generated (schemars behavior with Option fields)
Identify all config keys that may be missing from schema defaults (audit other Option<T> fields with skip_serializing_if)
Implement fix to ensure phase_overrides and other missing keys appear in schema default section
Add or update test to ensure schema completeness/correctness
Verify fix by running `make generate` and checking phase_overrides appears in agent.default
tasks.request: Validate how schemas/config.schema.json is generated. The phase_overrides section is not being shown in the schema and a file likely needs updated to ensure this and all config keys are properly captured.

tasks.title: Split crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs into cohesive modules
evidence: `rg --files -g '*.rs' crates/ralph/src | xargs wc -l | sort -nr | head -n 15` shows `2298 crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs`.
AGENTS.md states files over 1,000 LOC must be split unless explicitly justified in the thread.
plan: Identify distinct responsibilities inside the file and define a small module breakdown.
Move related types/functions into new module files under the same directory and wire them through a `mod` or `pub(crate)` exports.
Update imports and call sites to use the new module paths.
Add or adjust tests if behavior changes or if the split exposes missing coverage.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming all tests and linting still pass.
notes: Principles: Localize change (SoC/SRP), KISS.
Why it matters: oversized files reduce cohesion, slow reviews, and increase the risk of unrelated changes interacting.
Minimal fix: split into focused modules without changing external behavior or public APIs.
Acceptance: file is reduced below 1,000 LOC, responsibilities are separated, and `make ci` passes.

tasks.title: Implement subtask support for hierarchical task breakdown
evidence: No parent/child relationship fields in Task schema
Large tasks cannot be broken down into trackable subtasks
PRD multi-task mode creates sequential dependencies but not true subtasks
plan: Add `parent_id` field to Task for child-to-parent reference
Add `subtasks` array field or compute dynamically from parent_id
Add `ralph task subtask create <PARENT_ID> <REQUEST>` command
Update parent task progress based on subtask completion percentage
Block parent task completion until all subtasks are done
Update TUI to show collapsible subtask hierarchies
Update queue graph to show parent-child relationships
Add tests for subtask lifecycle and parent completion blocking

tasks.title: Add conditional task execution based on custom conditions
evidence: No conditional execution support in task schema
Cannot skip tasks based on file existence, env vars, or command output
All tasks in queue are attempted regardless of context
plan: Add `condition` field to Task with type and parameters
Support condition types: file_exists, env_set, command_success, task_status
Add `ralph task condition <TASK_ID> <TYPE> <PARAM>` command
Evaluate condition before task execution in run one/loop
Skip task with log message if condition not met
Add `--skip-conditions` flag to bypass condition checks
Add tests for each condition type

tasks.title: Implement IDE extensions for VS Code and JetBrains
evidence: No IDE extensions exist for Ralph
Users must switch between terminal and editor to check task status
No inline task creation from TODO comments in code
plan: Create VS Code extension with task tree view
Show current task, queue, and done tasks in sidebar
Add commands: run task, mark done, create task from selection
Highlight TODO/FIXME comments with quick-create task actions
Create JetBrains plugin with equivalent functionality
Support task status indicators in editor gutter
Add configuration for ralph binary path
Document installation and usage for both extensions
